description:
SpecRegistry::resolve() fast path (the common case) allocates a Vec<Descr> on
every call just to perform a HashMap lookup:

  if let Some(&id) = self.lookup.get(&(fn_id, input_descrs.to_vec())) {
      return Some(id);
  }

This is called at every Call, TailCall, and CallClosure codegen site. The
.to_vec() allocation is thrown away immediately after the lookup.

Fix: change the internal `lookup` field from:
  HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), SpecId>
to:
  HashMap<FnId, HashMap<Vec<Descr>, SpecId>>

This allows the fast path to use &[Descr] directly via Borrow:
  if let Some(&id) = self.lookup.get(&fn_id).and_then(|m| m.get(input_descrs)) {
      return Some(id);
  }

Vec<T> implements Borrow<[T]>, so HashMap<Vec<T>, V>::get accepts &[T] without
any allocation. No semantic change — the lookup produces the same SpecId.

Update register() and register_any_key_at() to insert into the two-level map:
  self.lookup.entry(fn_id).or_default().insert(input_descrs_vec, id);

Proven: existing SpecRegistry unit tests pass. All fixture tests pass.

## LANDING THE WORK
  Land this ticket before starting the next one:
  - Commit only the changes for this ticket, referencing fz-cg1.6
  - Close the ticket (`bw close fz-v9l.6`); It will tell you if work is newly unblocked
title: fz-cg1.6 fix SpecRegistry resolve fast-path: eliminate .to_vec() allocation
